title: while Loops
description: Repeat code while a condition is true.

slides.title: The while Loop
slides.content:
A `while` loop repeats as long as a condition remains TRUE.

Syntax:
```r
while (condition) {
  # code to repeat
}
```

Be careful: if the condition never becomes FALSE, you'll have an infinite loop!

slides.title: Simple while Loop

quizData.question: What is the risk of a while loop?

options.text: Infinite loop if condition never false

quizData.explanation: If the condition never becomes FALSE, the loop runs forever.

slides.title: When to Use while
slides.content: Use `while` when you don't know in advance how many iterations you need. For example, reading data until a condition is met, or implementing algorithms that converge.

slides.title: while Example: Approximate Square Root
slides.codeSnippet:
x <- 25
guess <- x/2
tolerance <- 0.0001
while (abs(guess^2 - x) > tolerance) {
  guess <- (guess + x/guess) / 2
}
print(guess)

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Always ensure the condition will eventually become FALSE. Update a variable inside the loop to move toward termination.
